subject: CSIT Policy Management
commit_message:
CSIT Policy Management

CSIT test case using FTC1 and applicable ONAP images

Change-Id: I7d3e382644f1b2e1de320931e24068976515cd4f

Issue-ID: CCSDK-2700

Signed-off-by: BjornMagnussonXA <bjorn.magnusson@est.tech>

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

(3 comments)

Some cosmetic comments on the Robot wrapper,but still +1 to confirm that this passed in my local environment too (not only as verified by Robot but I also had 104/104 successful testcases according to TC.log).
Copying script-based tests from another repository obviously won't fly as a long-term solution, but you have accumulated quite a mass of test cases here so it may take a while to translate these into Robot tests under ONAP repository.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1
> 
> (3 comments)
> 
> Some cosmetic comments on the Robot wrapper,but still +1 to confirm that this passed in my local environment too (not only as verified by Robot but I also had 104/104 successful testcases according to TC.log).
> Copying script-based tests from another repository obviously won't fly as a long-term solution, but you have accumulated quite a mass of test cases here so it may take a while to translate these into Robot tests under ONAP repository.

Thanks Lasse,
we will discuss in in the team how to remove the repo dependency as well as robotifying the test cases.
The current solution is just a quick and short term approach to get CSIT running.

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

No use rechecking this, the verify job is trying to find the plan from wrong directory (plans/oran/polmansuite/testplan.txt when it should be plans/ccsdk/oran/polmansuite/testplan.txt). That needs to be fixed in jjb and merged to make this work, I'll comment on the other review.

Patch Set 3:

> Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1
> 
> No use rechecking this, the verify job is trying to find the plan from wrong directory (plans/oran/polmansuite/testplan.txt when it should be plans/ccsdk/oran/polmansuite/testplan.txt). That needs to be fixed in jjb and merged to make this work, I'll comment on the other review.

The csit yaml file for ccsdk should be fixed as far as I understand. It was a bit unclear how to configure the jjb job in terms of project names etc. So the jjb should now specify 'ccsdk/oran' instead of just 'oran'.

Patch Set 9:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 9: Code-Review+1
> 
> As a bonus, now we also see the CSIT jobs under https://jenkins.onap.org/view/ccsdk-oran/ as well

Please take a look at rsync call in that job:

 rsync -av / /w/workspace/ccsdk-oran-master-csit-polmansuite/archives

Are directories set properly? Did you intend to sync whole root?

Patch Set 9:

(1 comment)

> Patch Set 9:
> 
> (1 comment)
> 
> > Patch Set 9: Code-Review+1
> > 
> > As a bonus, now we also see the CSIT jobs under https://jenkins.onap.org/view/ccsdk-oran/ as well
> 
> Please take a look at rsync call in that job:
> 
>  rsync -av / /w/workspace/ccsdk-oran-master-csit-polmansuite/archives
> 
> Are directories set properly? Did you intend to sync whole root?

I guess this comment was for Lasse K, not sure what it means since it seems to be related to the CSIT environment. But it is true that a repo is cloned into the archives dir during test execution (setup script) and the intention is to save this cloned repo for trouble shooting when test is complete, some important logs are created in the directory structure in that cloned repo.

Patch Set 9:

>  rsync -av / /w/workspace/ccsdk-oran-master-csit-polmansuite/archives
> 
> Are directories set properly? Did you intend to sync whole root?

You mean in https://jenkins.onap.org/view/ccsdk-oran/job/ccsdk-oran-master-csit-polmansuite/ ?
That is only a symptom of this commit having not yet been merged as that job takes the CSIT project from master, not from the review branch (the rsync part in completely in generic part of the CSIT and not in the hands of project-specific plans anyway). The job verifying this review branch is working correctly for rsync and everything else: https://jenkins.onap.org/view/ccsdk-oran/job/ccsdk-oran-master-verify-csit-polmansuite/ 

That said, the rsync in the generic part isn't really working sanely in the case of missing plan and I might do something to improve that (however, it's completely outside of the scope of this review)
